concerns.text: FR-001 and US-1 require ingesting 'NOAA/Copernicus reanalysis data' (temp, salinity, nutrients) alongside MODIS and in-situ data. Tasks T011-T014 only describe fetching SeaBASS (in-situ + ocean color) and aligning it. There is no task to ingest, align, or mask the external reanalysis grids required to satisfy FR-001's multi-modal requirement.

SC-001 requires determining if VLM R² exceeds baseline by a statistically significant margin (≥0.05) using an 'appropriate statistical test'. Tasks T017-T020 only cover training and computing metrics (RMSE, R², MAE). No task exists to perform the required statistical significance test (e.g., paired t-test, bootstrap) to validate the margin.

SC-005 requires reporting the variance in R² scores across basins and the difference between highest/lowest. Task T020 mentions generating a CSV with 'basin-stratified R² scores' but lacks an explicit task to calculate, aggregate, and output the specific variance/difference metric required by SC-005.

The spec assumes 'sufficient temporal overlap (≥ 10 years)' for stratified splits. Tasks T011-T014 focus on ingestion/alignment but lack a task to explicitly validate this 10-year overlap or implement the specific stratified temporal split logic required by the assumption before training begins.

Task T007 implements a 'versioning state manager' but does not clarify if it integrates with the 'Advancement-Evaluator Agent' logic mandated by Constitution Principle V (sole writer of current_stage). The task description is ambiguous regarding the specific agent integration required.
